name: Claude Mythos Preview
tagline: Anthropic's invitation-only early Mythos-class frontier model, run through Project Glasswing for cyberdefense work — later succeeded by Mythos 5 and Fable 5.
seoTitle: Claude Mythos Preview — Anthropic LLM Specs
metaDescription: Claude Mythos Preview is Anthropic's invitation-only Mythos-class model from April 2026, distributed via Project Glasswing for cybersecurity defense and later…
overview: Claude Mythos Preview is a general-purpose frontier model from Anthropic, the first member of its Claude Fable / Mythos (Mythos-class) line. Anthropic announced it on April 7, 2026, alongside Project Glasswing. Unlike Anthropic's widely released Opus and Sonnet models, Mythos Preview was never made generally available — Anthropic explicitly stated it did not plan to release it to the public, and distributed it only to vetted partners.

Mythos Preview is a broadly strong model that proved strikingly capable at computer-security tasks. Working through Project Glasswing, Anthropic and its partners used Claude Mythos Preview to find more than ten thousand high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across systemically important software, including zero-day flaws in every major operating system and every major web browser. Anthropic notes these cybersecurity abilities emerged as a byproduct of improvements to coding and reasoning rather than from training the model specifically for vulnerability discovery.

Claude Mythos Preview was succeeded on June 9, 2026 by Claude Mythos 5 (the same restricted Project Glasswing distribution) and Claude Fable 5 (the publicly available variant with extra safety classifiers). Anthropic says users find Mythos 5 comparable to — or somewhat stronger than — Mythos Preview in most cases, while costing substantially less.
strengths: Autonomous discovery of zero-day software vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers
Building working exploits by chaining multiple small attack primitives together
Reverse engineering closed-source binaries and turning known (N-day) flaws into functional exploits
Strong general-purpose coding and reasoning, from which its security capabilities emerged
Long-horizon, autonomous agentic security work over large codebases
useCases: Defensive vulnerability scanning of critical software and infrastructure codebases
Security research and exploit-chain analysis by vetted partners
Hardening systemically important open-source and commercial software
Cyberdefense programs run with infrastructure providers and government partners

links.label: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — Anthropic
Claude Mythos Preview on Vertex AI — Google Cloud
Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries — TechCrunch

pricing.note: Pricing quoted by Anthropic for access following the research-preview period, via the Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry. Its successors Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are priced lower, at $10/$50 per 1M tokens — described by Anthropic as less than half the price of Mythos Preview.

faq.q: Is Claude Mythos Preview available to the public?
faq.a: No. Anthropic stated it did not plan to make Claude Mythos Preview generally available. Access was invitation-only through Project Glasswing — initially around 40-50 partners (including Microsoft, Apple, Google, AWS, the Linux Foundation, Cisco, and Nvidia), later expanded to roughly 150-200 organizations across more than 15 countries.

faq.q: What is Claude Mythos Preview used for?
faq.a: It is a general-purpose model that proved exceptionally capable at cybersecurity. Project Glasswing partners used it defensively to scan critical codebases, where it found more than ten thousand high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities, including zero-days in every major operating system and web browser.

faq.q: How does Mythos Preview relate to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5?
faq.a: Mythos Preview is the first model in Anthropic's Mythos-class line. It was succeeded on June 9, 2026 by Mythos 5 (still restricted to Project Glasswing) and Fable 5 (the public variant with additional safety classifiers). Anthropic says Mythos 5 is comparable to or somewhat stronger than Mythos Preview, at substantially lower cost.

faq.q: How much did Claude Mythos Preview cost?
faq.a: Anthropic quoted $25 per million input tokens and $125 per million output tokens for access following the research-preview period, available via the Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry. The later Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models are priced lower at $10/$50 per million tokens.
